posts.hook: xAI's coding agent has been quietly phoning your whole codebase home
posts.caption: a grok cli quietly uploads entire local repos to google cloud regardless of settings, apple's openai suit traces back to a broken work friendship, and nvidia funds the very clouds that buy its own gpus. full digest at sift.yasint.dev (link in bio)

slides.title: xAI's Grok CLI uploads your whole repo, whether you asked or not
slides.desc: 12GB repo test: 5.1GB uploaded, model saw only 192KB, opt-out doesn't stop it

slides.title: Apple v OpenAI traces back to a broken work friendship
slides.desc: hardware chief Tang Tan's old boss John Ternus is now suing his hire

slides.title: Nvidia funds the clouds that buy its own GPUs
slides.desc: $2B into CoreWeave, $2B into Nebius, both spend it straight back on chips

slides.title: ClickHouse quadrupled Postgres connection throughput
slides.desc: one PgBouncer process to a fleet: 87k to 336k transactions per second

slides.title: SQLite's STRICT tables finally get a toggle in sqlite-utils
slides.desc: STRICT rejects wrong-typed inserts; 4.1 adds a flag to switch it on

slides.title: FCC approves a mirror satellite to beam sunlight down at night
slides.desc: first of a planned 50,000-satellite fleet; astronomers warn of tripled sky glow

terms.gloss: the US agency that regulates airwaves and satellite licenses
